channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Late Night Breakfast This Thursday April 30th!
item.description: Join us for a Decades Themed Late-Night Breakfast this Thursday, April 30th from 9–11 PM at Commons Dining Hall! Come dressed in your favorite era, whether it’s groovy ’70s, neon ’80s, or early 2000s vibes, and enjoy a fun night of food, music, and nostalgia. All forms of payment accepted for entry. If you have any questions you can email dining@rpi.edu.
item.author: Students

item.title: Biomedical Engineering Weinbaum Lecture
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 30, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Join BME for our endowned Weinbaum lecture as Dr. Rosa Krajmalnik-Brown from Arizona State University presents “Heal the Gut to Help the Brain: Microbiota Transplant Therapy in Autism”
item.author: Community

item.title: Connected Dreams: Advanced Digital Imaging Exhibition
item.description: You are cordially invited to Connected Dreams, an exhibition of our Advanced Digital Imaging class short study works in progress. The exhibition is in the Sage Labs Dean’s Gallery and extends to the Vertical Galleries (near the elevators on each floor) and sections of the Corridors of Creativity near Sage 2411. Please bring your mobile devices to experience the AR.
item.author: Community

item.title: Call for Fall 2026 NRB Beyond Blocks
item.description: Through Beyond Blocks, students participate in open houses, club fairs, workshops, and social events that help them stay connected, get involved, and thrive in their new environment. Submit an event: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_ers5JWvVJ7QSmjQ Questions? Contact fye@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: North American Conference on Video Game Music
item.description: The North American Conference on Video Game Music (NACVGM) returns May 1–2, 2026 as a hybrid event hosted at RPI at EMPAC. The conference features a dynamic program of scholarly papers, panels, and creative work, alongside performances and opportunities for community engagement. Participants present 20- and 10-minute talks, poster sessions, and demonstrations of game and sound art, reflecting the breadth of contemporary approaches to game audio research and practice.
item.author: Community

item.title: TVCOG RPI Night | April 28
item.description: Join the RPI community for an evening at the Tech Valley Center of Gravity, sponsored by the RPI Lally School of Management, from 6–8 PM. Explore a 14,000 sq. ft. makerspace, connect with local alumni, and enjoy free pizza. Open to all students and located near the Blitman shuttle stop. RSVP via the link above.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Department of Economics Spring Seminar Series: Kyunghoon Ban, Rochester Institute of Technology
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Kyunghoon Ban, who will present: “Difference-in-Differences Models in the Presence of Mediators” next Wednesday, April 29th at 3:30 PM in SAGE 5510.
item.author: Community

item.title: Joint Cognitive Science and Computer Science Colloquium with Dr. Ora Lassila
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science and Computer Science Departments on Wednesday, April 29th, at 12pm in Carnegie 113 for a seminar by Dr. Ora Lassila titled “Symbolic AI in the Age of LLMs”. Pizza and refreshments will be provided.
item.author: Community

item.title: Announcing RPI Reads: A Summer Reading Initiative
item.description: RPI Libraries is excited to launch RPI Reads, a summer reading initiative for the entire RPI community! Register to participate and log any reading you complete this summer—whether for academic work or pure enjoyment. Every entry counts, and participants will be entered into a raffle for prizes. You can take part from anywhere, either in person or virtually. Visit the Folsom Library Service Desk or the RPI Reads website for full details and to get started!
item.author: Community

item.title: Teaching and Learning Collaboratory 2026 Spring In-Person RPI Faculty and Graduate TA’s Workshop: Teaching and Mentoring with Neurodivergence in Mind
item.description:
In this session, faculty are introduced to a greater understanding of neurodivergence in college students. The program clarifies what neurodivergence is, what it isn’t, and offers practical tools for faculty that support structured, high-expectation learning environments where neurodivergent students can succeed. We’ll focus on strategies and concepts that support the maintenance of academic rigor while reducing unnecessary barriers to learning.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
